shape_semantics: code cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,W,L,W: value_profile AND naive resolve, value_profile_cmplog BLOCKS, cmplog non-decisive. ANTI-SYNERGY -- adding the I2S stage to value_profile (vpc = both techniques) HURTS: vpc loses to vp-alone and to naive. The verification question for every branch is WHY the extra I2S delta blocks what vp-alone resolves.

hypotheses.label: vpc's I2S substitution pins the corpus to a WRONG decoy literal (sibling case / enumerated case / valid-range boundary), flooding the loser corpus with a value that blocks the target arm

params.decoy_source: the sibling / enumerated-case / boundary literal named in why_loser_doesnt (e.g. MONGOLIAN/NKO tag for 5761, 0x11481 sibling for 6124_h0, the 0x0020/0x00A0/0x2006 case literals for 6263, the GSUB/GPOS table tags for 6162/6210, 0x20 space-class for 5683)
params.target_value: the W-resolving operand named in why_winner_satisfies

hypotheses.prediction: vpc's corpus is ENRICHED for the decoy literal relative to vp (decoy_enrich > 0) while NOT enriched for the target value (signed_target_enrich <= 0). G1-discriminating: pure overhead/throughput drain (the rival hypothesis) predicts NO directional byte-frequency difference between vpc and vp corpora at the gate offset (decoy_enrich ~ 0) -- it slows the loser without steering it to any specific value. G2-independent: it scores corpus BYTE frequencies at the gate offset, not the vpc resolve/block outcome that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP Hamming gradient lands ON the exact 4-byte switch-case codepoint while vpc's added I2S stage diverts mutation onto sibling operands and never closes the last bits to the exact literal -- a closeness gap, not a wrong-value-floods-corpus gap

params.operand_literal: 0x0E36 for 5623 (width 4, LE bytes 36 0e 00 00); 0x1148D for 6124_h0 (width 4, LE bytes 8d 14 01 00 -- the primary winner-seed literal of the 0x1148B/0x1148D case pair)

hypotheses.prediction: value_profile's resolving corpus lands at or essentially at the exact operand (vp_min_distance ~ 0) while value_profile_cmplog's blocking corpus stalls measurably short of it (vpc_min_distance > vp_min_distance, so distance_gap > 0 with vp the closer arm). G1-discriminating: this separates the GRADIENT-CLOSENESS mechanism from the decoy-overcommit mechanism the prior test already tried and FAILED on these two branches -- decoy_overcommit predicts the vpc corpus is byte-enriched at the gate offset for a NAMED wrong literal (decoy_enrich high); the closeness mechanism instead predicts vpc simply ratchets LESS CLOSE to the target literal (no specific decoy needs to dominate, only that vpc's nearest-window Hamming distance to the exact operand stays larger than vp's). It also rules out pure overhead-drain, which predicts NO closeness gap (vpc would reach the literal just as near, only slower). G2-independent: it measures the minimum Hamming distance from corpus windows to the gate operand, computed from saved seed bytes -- never the vpc resolve/block label that defined the shape.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap > 0.15 AND vp_min_distance < 0.1

non_discriminable.label: vp's gradient incrementally ASSEMBLES a multi-glyph / multi-field structure (Indic consonant+joiner syllable) and vpc's I2S operand-substitution disrupts that assembly -- there is no single fixed-offset literal or single gate operand whose closeness can be measured, so no built tool discriminates assembly-disruption from generic overhead-drain
non_discriminable.why_non_discriminable: harfbuzz_5865 (do-while loop-exit over an assembled Indic consonant-syllable cluster, gate_structure=state_or_grammar, operand_kind=none_structural, byte_signature=dispersed_multibyte) has NO fixed-offset value gate -> operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature and value_distance_reached has no single operand_literal to target. The natural candidate is joint_necessity's struct_size_and_token_count, but that tool is built for the JOINT vpc-RESOLVING / cmplog-BLOCKING assembly-depth subtype (vpc needs both techniques to build a LARGER structure); here the polarity is INVERTED -- vpc BLOCKS and vp resolves, so 'vpc builds a larger structure' is the wrong direction and its metrics (size_lift, vp_tags, vpc_tags) cannot attribute the block to assembly-disruption vs. throughput drain. An equally-sized vp-resolving and vpc-blocking glyph stream is consistent with BOTH disruption-of-assembly and pure I2S overhead, and the dispersed (non-contiguous) glyph encoding has no countable target token to enrich. G3 honest fallback: inconclusive.

non_discriminable.label: non-literal / derived-relation gate: I2S has no input-byte literal to substitute, so vpc pays pure CmpLog per-exec overhead and loses on throughput -- no value-level signature to measure
non_discriminable.why_non_discriminable: bloaty_301 (start>=end pointer-arithmetic relation, none_structural) and libpng_4072_h1 (derived idat_size length field) have NO fixed-offset input literal for I2S to substitute toward, so operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature and value_distance_reached has no operand by construction. The only candidate signal is global corpus-scale scheduling (vpc corpus inflated/throttled vs vp), which is a per-trial quantity confounded by every other branch and by raw exec count -- it cannot attribute a per-branch block to the I2S delta, and an inflated corpus is equally consistent with benign exploration. data_realities: I2S is untagged in mutation_op and value_profile is a feedback, so NO seed_lineage 'I2S-overhead event' is measurable. G3 honest fallback: inconclusive.

corpus_coverage_gap.label: I2S substitution steers the vpc loser corpus toward valid ICC headers whose version major byte stays <= 0x09, depleting the >0x09 target arm -- a clean fixed-offset (offset 0x08) inequality decoy gate that operand_enrichment WOULD score, but the branch is seed-starved on this server and its corpus lives on sB
corpus_coverage_gap.note: lcms_2137 is a single-byte (width 1) inequality gate at file offset 0x08 with operand 0x09; mechanism = I2S pins corpus to valid small version bytes (<=0x09 decoy) starving the >0x09 winner arm -- exactly the decoy_overcommit_wrong_literal signature with an anti/depletion polarity (decoy_enrich for the valid-small-version byte). It is NOT marked decidable:false: per the cross-server rule, lcms is an sB target and this branch is flagged seed_starved (W=2,L=24; rank-10 re-bisect candidate). The owning server arbitrates after re-bisecting >=3 W seeds. operand_enrichment is the correct descriptor; width-1 chance-alignment caveat applies but the >0x09-vs-<=0x09 split is a magnitude (inequality) distinction, not a single-byte equality coincidence.

data_realities_respected: on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 -> the decoy and closeness tests score ONLY the harfbuzz branches; bloaty_301, lcms_2137, libpng_4072_h1 have no corpus on s4 and route to their owning server (sB) or fallback.

operand_enrichment needs a fixed-offset VALUE gate (skips structural/length) and >=3 W and >=3 L seeds; value_distance_reached needs one operand_literal and on-disk seeds, and flags width<=2 chance-alignment (both new-test operands are width 4, safe).
I2S and value_profile are both invisible in mutation_op (no lineage event) -- no overhead-drain hypothesis is given a lineage descriptor; the non-literal gates fall to decidable:false honestly.
harfbuzz_5865 is a dispersed-multibyte structural-assembly gate with inverted polarity vs joint_necessity's assembly-depth subtype, so no built tool discriminates it -> decidable:false.

lcms_2137 is NOT marked decidable:false despite seed-starvation/corpus absence on s4 -- it is an sB target with a genuinely discriminable fixed-offset decoy gate; the owning server arbitrates after re-bisecting (cross-server rule).
notes: ROUND-1 RE-DESIGN (loop-until-dry). KEPT verbatim (frozen, superset): decoy_overcommit_wrong_literal (operand_enrichment) -- confirmed on >=1 of its harfbuzz members; carried forward unchanged so the 6 confirmed branches re-validate. NEW mechanism for the UNLABELED gradient-climb branches 5623 and 6124_h0: vp_gradient_reaches_exact_literal_i2s_stalls_short, measured by the previously-UNTRIED value_distance_reached tool. Rationale for the new hypothesis being genuinely different (G1): the prior decoy test (operand_enrichment, decoy_enrich/signed_target_enrich) was SCORED on these two and the rule did NOT hold (rule_not_met) -- because their mechanism is not a wrong-literal that FLOODS the corpus but a CLOSENESS shortfall: value_profile's Hamming-gradient ratchets onto the exact 4-byte codepoint (0x0E36 / 0x1148D) while vpc's I2S diverts onto sibling operands and stalls a measurable Hamming distance short. value_distance_reached directly measures that vp_min_distance ~ 0 vs vpc_min_distance > 0 (distance_gap, winner_closer), discriminating closeness-shortfall from both decoy-flood (decoy_enrich) and pure overhead-drain (which predicts no closeness gap). DECIDABLE:FALSE (G3 honest inconclusive): harfbuzz_5865 (dispersed structural Indic-syllable assembly, no single operand and inverted polarity for joint_necessity), bloaty_301 (pointer start>=end relation, no literal), libpng_4072_h1 (derived idat_size length field, no literal) -- all lack a fixed-offset value or single gate operand and have only a confounded global corpus-scale signal. CORPUS-COVERAGE-GAP (NOT decidable:false): lcms_2137 -- a clean single-byte >0x09 decoy gate operand_enrichment would score, but seed-starved and sB-owned; deferred to the owning server per the cross-server rule. G2 throughout: every test scores corpus byte frequencies / corpus-window Hamming distances, independent of the vpc resolve pattern that defined the shape.
